title: Remove Industry Growth section from inputs and outputs

requirements.text: Remove the Industry Growth input field/section from the Company Snapshot form so users can no longer view or edit an industry growth value.
acceptanceCriteria: The Company Snapshot form no longer renders an input labeled "Industry Growth (%)".
No validation error can reference the removed Industry Growth field.
The example and default input sets no longer include an Industry Growth value in the UI.

requirements.text: Remove Industry Growth from the shareable-link (URL query parameter) encoding/decoding so generated links no longer include an industry growth parameter and parsing ignores any existing industry growth parameter without breaking.
acceptanceCriteria: Newly generated shareable links do not include the industry growth query parameter (currently named "indGrowth").
If a user opens an older link that contains "indGrowth", the app still loads successfully and ignores that value (or safely defaults it) without runtime errors.

requirements.text: Update the output calculations and Output Values UI copy so Industry Growth is not included anywhere in the Actual Value computation or its displayed formula/description.
acceptanceCriteria: The "Actual Value" button subtitle no longer mentions Growth (e.g., it reflects "Capital + Assets ÷ Shares" or equivalent).
The "Actual Value Per Share" description no longer mentions "Industry Growth".
The Actual Value calculation no longer depends on an Industry Growth input value; the app builds and runs with the Industry Growth field removed from the inputs model.
No part of the results UI displays a value labeled or derived from "Industry Growth".

constraints: Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui or any paths listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Keep all changes within the existing single React app; no new backend services or external databases.
nonGoals: Do not change backend Motoko logic (backend/main.mo) unless strictly required for compilation.
Do not add new metrics, new sections, or redesign unrelated parts of the form/results beyond removing Industry Growth references.
